examples.text: وَيَأْتِي ٱلْعِلْمُ ٱلرَّبَّانِيُّ ٱلْإِلٰهِيُّ فَيَصِيرُ قَمِيصًا عَلَى قَلْبِكَ وَسِرِّكَ
examples.english: Then divine knowledge settles in, and it becomes a robe around your heart and your core.

examples.text: مَا كَانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْكِتَابَ وَٱلْحُكْمَ وَٱلنُّبُوَّةَ ثُمَّ يَقُولَ لِلنَّاسِ كُونُوا عِبَادًا لِي مِنْ دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلٰكِن كُونُوا رَبَّانِيِّينَ بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تُعَلِّمُونَ ٱلْكِتَابَ وَبِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَدْرُسُونَ
examples.english: No man whom God gifts with scriptures, wisdom, and prophecy would say to people, "Be worshipers of me, not God," and not "Be godly, through what you have taught about the scriptures and what you have studied."

etymology.text: Relative adjective (nisba) composed of رَبّ (rabb, “lord; god”) + ـَانـ (-ān-) + ـِيّ (-iyy).

body.text: One who devotes himself to religious services
applies himself to acts of devotion;
who possesses a knowledge of God:
(T:) or the first signifies, (M,) or signifies also, (K,) and so the second, (M,)
a learned man, a teacher of others, who nourishes people with the small matters of knowledge,
a learned man firmly grounded in knowledge,
a learned man who practices what he knows and instructs others:
one of high rank in knowledge,
learned with respect to what is lawful and what is unlawful, and what is commanded and what is forbidden:
meaning “ God: ” (TA, and some copies of the K:) the
being added to give intensiveness to the signification; (M;) or, as Sb says, to denote a special reference to the knowledge of the
Lord, as though the word signified
one possessing a knowledge of the Lord exclusively of other branches of knowledge;
, (T, M, and so in some copies of the K,) meaning “ long-bearded, ” (T,) or “ largebearded, ” (M,) and
, “ having much hair: ” (T:) or it is a Syriac word; (TA, and some copies of the K;) or Hebrew; and was unknown to the [pagan] Arabs, and known only to the men of law and science: (TA:) the pl. is
, (T, S,) occurring in the Kur iii. 73 (S) [and v. 48 and 68].
